member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditSeverity
member.summary: Severity level of an audit event.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditEvent
member.summary: Immutable record of a single auditable event in an agent session. All sensitive fields are masked before recording.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditEvent.ToString
member.summary: Produces a single-line structured log representation.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail
member.summary:
Thread-safe, append-only audit trail for agent sessions.
The AuditTrail automatically: - Masks secrets in all recorded content (via ) - Attaches session correlation from - Exposes typed queries for security analysis (e.g., all security events, failures only)
Design rule: once written, events are immutable and cannot be deleted. This is a deliberate security property — audit trails must be tamper-evident.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.Record(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditSeverity,MonadicSharp.Error,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Records an agent action with automatic secret masking.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.RecordSecurityViolation(System.String,System.String,MonadicSharp.Error,System.String)
member.summary: Records a security violation (always severity=Security, succeeded=false).

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.Events
member.summary: All recorded events, in chronological order.

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.SecurityEvents
member.summary: Only security-level events.

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.Failures
member.summary: Only failed events.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.ForAgent(System.String)
member.summary: Events from a specific agent.

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.HasSecurityViolations
member.summary: True if any security violation has been recorded in this session.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Audit.AuditTrail.ExportReport
member.summary: Exports all events as a structured text report.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Core.SecureAgentContext
member.summary:
An enhanced with security services: a , an , and a .
This is the entry point for the MonadicSharp.Security layer. Instead of creating bare AgentContexts, use to get a context that automatically validates inputs and records all operations.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Core.SecureAgentContext.ValidateInput(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Validates user input through the PromptGuard and records the check in the audit trail. Returns the safe input on success, or a typed SecurityError on injection detection.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Core.SecureAgentContext.Narrow(MonadicSharp.Agents.Core.AgentCapability)
member.summary: Narrows capabilities for a child agent while preserving audit/guard context.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Errors.SecurityError
member.summary: Typed security error factory. All security violations surface as values in Result<T> — no silent failures, no unhandled exceptions.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuard
member.summary:
Detects and optionally sanitizes prompt injection attempts before they reach a language model or agent pipeline.
MonadicSharp.Security's PromptGuard follows the same Railway-Oriented pattern as the rest of the framework: validation failures are typed values in a , never exceptions.
Usage:
var guard = PromptGuard.Default; var result = guard.Validate(userInput); result.Match( onSuccess: safe => pipeline.RunAsync(safe, ctx), onFailure: error => Result<Output>.Failure(error));

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuard.Default
member.summary: Default guard with standard injection detection rules enabled.

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuard.Strict
member.summary: Strict guard with extended ruleset and tighter limits.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuard.Validate(System.String)
member.summary: Validates the input against all configured injection rules. Returns the original input on success (ready for forwarding to an agent), or a typed on the first rule violation.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuard.Sanitize(System.String)
member.summary: Sanitizes the input by removing or replacing detected injection patterns. Use when you want to proceed with cleaned input rather than blocking entirely.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Guard.PromptGuardOptions
member.summary: Configuration for .

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker
member.summary:
Prevents secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings) from leaking into agent traces, audit logs, or LLM responses.
The masker works in two modes: 1. Pattern-based: detects well-known secret shapes (AWS keys, JWTs, Bearer tokens, etc.) 2. Registry-based: masks specific known values registered at startup
All masking is lossless — the masked string retains its length structure so logs remain readable while secrets are replaced with [MASKED].

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.Register(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a known secret value to be masked wherever it appears.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.AddPattern(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a custom secret detection pattern.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.Mask(System.String)
member.summary: Masks all detected secrets in the input string. Returns the sanitized string safe for logging/tracing.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.ContainsSecret(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the input contains any detectable secret pattern.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.MaskDictionary(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Masks a dictionary of values (e.g., agent metadata, headers).

member.name: P:MonadicSharp.Security.Masking.SecretMasker.Default
member.summary: Singleton default instance — no known secrets registered.

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Middleware.SecureAgentWrapper`2
member.summary:
Wraps any with a security layer that: 1. Validates input through the before executing 2. Masks secrets in the output before returning 3. Records all executions (success and failure) in the 4. Enforces capability checks against the context
This is the adapter pattern for retrofitting security onto existing agents without modifying them — the wrapped agent is unaware of the security layer.
Usage:
IAgent<string, Summary> secureAgent = mySummaryAgent.WithSecurity(secureCtx); var result = await secureAgent.ExecuteAsync(userInput, secureCtx.Context);

member.name: T:MonadicSharp.Security.Middleware.SecureAgentExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for wrapping agents with security.

member.name: M:MonadicSharp.Security.Middleware.SecureAgentExtensions.WithSecurity``2(MonadicSharp.Agents.IAgent{``0,``1},MonadicSharp.Security.Core.SecureAgentContext,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Wraps an agent with the full security middleware stack.
